pressures: Invasive understorey (Sasa bamboo, Kudzu on former base land)
Port disturbance — noise and air quality (Honmoku Pier ~1.8 km)
Isolated from nearest green corridor (Negishi Forest ~2.3 km SW)
Low observer effort (2.3 obs / km path)

interventions.title: Remove invasive understorey
interventions.description: Manual removal of Sasa bamboo and Kudzu on the north slope. Allows native shrub regeneration within 2-3 seasons. Priority area: 0.2 ha around the hilltop bench.

interventions.title: Plant a stepping-stone corridor
interventions.description: A street-tree row along Honmoku-dori toward Negishi Forest Park (2.3 km SW) would reduce isolation. Priority segment: Wadayama to Maeyama junction (~800 m). Estimated connectivity gain: +18% network betweenness.

interventions.title: Introduce native understorey species
interventions.description: Plant Aucuba japonica, Camellia japonica, and native ferns in the gaps left after Sasa removal. Focus on species used as food and shelter by Honmoku's resident thrush population.

interventions.title: Maintain twice-yearly mowing
interventions.description: The Oct 2025 shift to biannual mowing has already improved the trend. Continue and extend to the full park boundary (currently only the hilltop bench area).

interventions.title: Bioblitz with Honmoku elementary school
interventions.description: Observer effort is 2.3 obs/km — below the Naka Ward average of 4.1. A school survey day would improve the biodiversity baseline and engage the local community.
